input.prompt:
My .NET MAUI Android build on macOS shows a 'JAVA_HOME is not set' warning.
I found a Stack Overflow answer telling me to add
`export JAVA_HOME=/Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/temurin-17.jdk/Contents/Home`
to my shell profile. Is this the right fix? What should I actually do?

input.initialPrompt:
My .NET MAUI Android build on macOS shows a 'JAVA_HOME is not set' warning.
I found a Stack Overflow answer telling me to add
`export JAVA_HOME=/Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/temurin-17.jdk/Contents/Home`
to my shell profile. Is this the right fix? What should I actually do?

data.content:
That SO answer will work but is **fragile**—it hardcodes a specific JDK vendor and version. Better approaches:

### Recommended fix

Use macOS's built-in `/usr/libexec/java_home` utility. Add this to your `~/.zshrc` (or `~/.bash_profile`):

```sh
export JAVA_HOME=$(/usr/libexec/java_home -v 17)
```

This automatically resolves to whichever JDK 17 is installed (Temurin, Microsoft, Oracle, etc.) and survives JDK updates.

### Why the SO answer is risky

- If you switch to Microsoft's OpenJDK (which .NET MAUI recommends), the path breaks.
- If you update from 17.0.x to a new patch via Homebrew, the directory name may change.

### After editing your profile

```sh
source ~/.zshrc        # apply without restarting terminal
echo $JAVA_HOME        # verify it resolved correctly
dotnet build -t:Run -f net8.0-android   # rebuild
```

### If you don't have a JDK 17 installed yet

The .NET MAUI docs recommend Microsoft's build of OpenJDK:

```sh
brew install --cask microsoft-openjdk@17
```
data.reasoningText: The user is asking about a .NET MAUI Android build issue on macOS where JAVA_HOME isn't set. They found a Stack Overflow answer suggesting hardcoding a path. Let me give them practical advice without needing to look anything up—this is general developer knowledge.
